questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Recently, in an unfortunate development, more than 100 employees were killed when the police resorted to lathi charge on a crowd.
Courses of action: I. An enquiry commission should be set up to investigate the avoidable reasons of the incident.
II. The police officer on duty should immediately be suspended.
III. The police trainees should be given a course on crowd control and group psychology.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The fiscal deficit of the country is not likely to be contained within the 6.5% limit as prescribed by the IMF.
Courses of action: I. Efforts should be made to reduce the fiscal deficit.
II. Efforts should be made not to let the fiscal deficit grow anymore.
III. Efforts should be made not to allow IMF to interfere in our internal economic matters.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: It has been observed that out of every 10 students who drop out of school before class X, eight are female students.
Courses of action: I. Efforts should be made to reduce the aggregate number of drop-outs at school level.
II. An awareness programme should be undertaken to tell people about the importance of female education.
III. Female education should be made legally compulsory with punishable violations.

choices.c: I and either II or III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: An official of the sports committee has been accused of corruption and favouritism.
Courses of action: I. The committee should dismiss the official immediately.
II. The committee should dismiss the allegation as mischievous and baseless.
III. The committee should suspend the official for the time being.

choices.e: Any one of I, II or III follows

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: It has now been proved that most of the cases of leprosy are not infectious and that they are curable.

choices.a: An awareness drive should be launched among people regarding this fact
choices.b: People affected by leprosy should not to boycott leprosy patients from the society
choices.c: Leprosy should be declared a risk-free disease by the government

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The demand for automobiles has slumped indicating a possible recession in the automobile sector.
Courses of action: I. The government should announce tax reliefs to the automobile sector.
II. The automobile sector should reduce the prices of automobiles.
III. The industrialists should look for some better business ventures than automobiles.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The neighbours suspect that the murderer could be one of the relatives of the deceased.
Courses of action: I. The relatives of the deceased should be interrogated.
II. The neighbours should be prosecuted for defamation.
III. The relatives of the deceased should be punished.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The red-tapism in our official work has become a nuisance.
Courses of action: I. The government should try to minimise the paper work.
II. The officials should be directed not to sit on files without justifiable reasons.
III. The official staff should be asked to remove red-tapism within the next ten days.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The demand for Indian garments in the foreign countries has registered a sharp growth.
Courses of action: I. An enquiry commission should be set up to examine the causes of this growth.
II. Garment manufacturing industries should follow strict quality control programmes.
III. Export of Indian garments should be banned.

choices.e: Either I or I and III follow
